Strong bond and mutual respect; work together in battle, with the dog playing an active role.

events.name: Magical Era Begins
events.description: If you’ll believe me there was a time when the fairies were none so shy as they are now. That was the time when beasts talked to men, when there were spells and enchantments and magic every day, when there was great store of hidden treasure to be dug up, and adventures for the asking.
thoughts: The text describes a past time when magic was common and adventures were plentiful. This sets up the world before any conflict or disruption has occurred.
The event text describes a past time when magic and adventure were common, establishing the world before any conflict or disruption has occurred. This matches option 1: setup.

events.name: Old Man and Woman Live Alone
events.description: At that time, you must know, an old man and an old woman lived alone by themselves. They were good and they were poor and they had no children at all.
thoughts: The event describes the world or the hero before any conflict or disruption has occurred. The old man and woman are presented as a baseline state without any threat or lack.

The text describes a peaceful scene where an old man and woman live alone, with no mention of conflict or disruption. This fits the description of 'setup' as it presents the hero's ordinary life before any conflict has occurred.

events.name: Old Couple's Morning Routine
events.description: One fine day, “What are you doing this morning, good man?” says the old woman. “Oh,” says the old man, “I’m off to the mountains with my billhook to gather a faggot of sticks for our fire. And what are you doing, good wife?” “Oh,” says the old woman, “I’m off to the stream to wash clothes. It’s my washing day,” she adds.
thoughts: The event describes the ordinary life of an old man and an old woman before any conflict or disruption has occurred. It presents their daily routine without any threat, villain, or lack present yet.
The text describes the ordinary life of an old man and his wife before any conflict or disruption has occurred. The scene focuses on their daily routine and chores.

The provided text describes the ordinary life of the old man and his wife before any conflict or disruption has occurred. It sets up their daily routine, showing what they do on a typical day, without introducing any threat or villainy.

actions: is off to the stream to wash clothes
is off to the mountains with his billhook to gather a faggot of sticks for their fire

events.name: Old Woman Finds Floating Peach
events.description: So the old man went to the mountains and the old woman went to the stream. Now, while she was washing the clothes, what should she see but a fine ripe peach that came floating down the stream? The peach was big enough, and rosy red on both sides.
thoughts: The story continues with a peaceful and mundane scene, where the old woman washes clothes by the stream. The introduction of the peach floating down the stream creates an element of surprise and possibly foreshadowing, but it doesn't escalate the conflict or tension.

The event is a calm and peaceful scene where the old woman finds a peach floating down the stream. Given that this is early in the narrative (event 3/24), it's likely part of the setup or initial situation section. The context provided earlier establishes the setting, characters, and their daily routine, making this event consistent with the 'initial_situation' category.

actions: was washing clothes by the stream
saw a fine ripe peach floating down the stream

events.name: She Finds Lucky Peach for Him
events.description: “I’m in luck this morning,” said the dame, and she pulled the peach to shore with a split bamboo stick. By-and-by, when her good man came home from the hills, she set the peach before him. “Eat, good man,” she said; “this is a lucky peach I found in the stream and brought home for you.”
thoughts: The event presents a peaceful scene where an old woman brings home a peach as a gift for her husband. This is a continuation of the setup phase, showing the ordinary life of the couple before any conflict or disruption has occurred.
The event describes the old woman's ordinary life: her daily routine and tasks. She is preparing for her washing day, which indicates a stable state before any conflict or disruption has occurred.

The event describes the old woman finding a peach in the stream while washing clothes. This action doesn't drive the story forward with conflict or introduce a problem; it's part of setting up the ordinary life and establishing the characters' daily routine.

actions: brought home a peach as a gift for her husband

events.name: Peach Transforms into Baby Boy
events.description: But the old man never got a taste of the peach. And why did he not? All of a sudden the peach burst in two and there was no stone to it, but a fine boy baby where the stone should have been.
thoughts: The event introduces a magical element that upsets the ordinary life of the old couple. The peach's sudden transformation into a baby replaces the expected stone, creating an extraordinary situation.

actions: did not get a taste of the peach

events.name: Peach Transforms into Baby Boy
events.description: “Mercy me!” says the old woman. “Mercy me!” says the old man. The boy baby first ate up one half of the peach and then he ate up the other half. When he had done this he was finer and stronger than ever.
thoughts: The event describes a magical or extraordinary situation, specifically an unexpected transformation of a peach into a baby. This is not part of the main conflict or action yet; it's more of an astonishing occurrence that sets up the story.

The event describes a miraculous birth, where an old woman finds a peach with no stone and instead discovers a baby. This marks the beginning of a new stage in the story, introducing a key character and creating a sense of wonder.

actions: ate up one half of the peach
ate up the other half of the peach
found a peach with no stone instead of a baby

events.name: Baby Born from Peach
events.description: “Momotaro! Momotaro!” cries the old man; “the eldest son of the peach.” “Truth it is indeed,” says the old woman; “he was born in a peach.”
thoughts: The event describes a miraculous birth where a boy baby emerges from a peach. This event occurs at the beginning of the story and sets up the protagonist's origins.

The narrative is setting up the hero's origin story, specifically explaining how Momotaro was born from a magical peach. This event presents the ordinary life of the old man and old woman before the extraordinary incident occurs.

events.name: Momotaro Grows into Manhood
events.description: Both of them took such good care of Momotaro that soon he was the stoutest and bravest boy of all that country-side. He was a credit to them, you may believe. The neighbours nodded their heads and they said, “Momotaro is the fine young man!”
thoughts: The event describes the hero's ordinary life: their home, family, social role, or daily routine. Momotaro has grown strong and brave under the care of his adoptive parents, and he is now a fine young man according to the neighbours.
The narrative context suggests that Momotaro's upbringing and development are being highlighted. The neighbors' positive opinion of him indicates a stable and peaceful situation.

The event describes Momotaro's ordinary life and his positive relationship with the old man and woman who raised him. The neighbors acknowledge him as a fine young man, which suggests that he has already reached a certain level of maturity and respect in the community.

actions: was taken care of by the old woman and man

events.name: Momotaro Requests Millet Dumplings.
events.description: “Mother,” says Momotaro one day to the old woman, “make me a good store of kimi-dango” (which is the way that they call millet dumplings in those parts).
thoughts: The hero, Momotaro, requests food from his mother, which is a typical action for someone about to embark on a journey. This event fits the category of 'preparation' as it shows the hero acquiring what they need before facing challenges.

events.name: Momotaro Asks for Provisions
events.description: “What for do you want kimi-dango?” says his mother. “Why,” says Momotaro, “I’m going on a journey, or as you may say, an adventure, and I shall be needing the kimi-dango on the way.”
thoughts: The event describes the hero's request for a specific item, kimi-dango, before embarking on his journey. This aligns with the preparation phase, as Momotaro is acquiring what he needs to face challenges ahead.

actions: provides the requested item, kimi-dango to Momotaro

events.name: Momotaro Requests Food Supply
events.description: “Where are you going, Momotaro?” says his mother. “I’m off to the Ogres’ Island,” says Momotaro, “to get their treasure, and I should be obliged if you’d let me have the kimi-dango as soon as may be,” he says.
thoughts: The event is a request from Momotaro to his mother for kimi-dango before embarking on his journey to the Ogres' Island. This event marks the beginning of Momotaro's adventure and shows him preparing for the challenges ahead.

The event describes the hero's preparation for a journey and their request for food that will be needed on the way. This is an instance of the hero requesting something essential before embarking on a quest.

actions: Preparing for the journey by asking for food

events.name: Momotaro Prepares to Leave Home
events.description: So they made him the kimi-dango, and he put them in a wallet, and he tied the wallet to his girdle and off he set. “Sayonara, and good luck to you, Momotaro!” cried the old man and the old woman. “Sayonara! Sayonara!” cried Momotaro.
thoughts: The event describes the hero's preparation for his journey by acquiring a necessary object, which marks a turning point in the story as he sets off towards the main conflict.
The event describes Momotaro preparing for his journey by receiving kimi-dango from his mother. This is a specific action taken by the hero before facing the main conflict on Ogres' Island.

events.name: Momotaro Departs Island
events.description: He hadn’t gone far when he fell in with a monkey. “Kia! Kia!” says the monkey. “Where are you off to, Momotaro?” Says Momotaro, “I’m off to the Ogres’ Island for an adventure.”
thoughts: The event shows the hero leaving his familiar environment to begin the journey or mission. The previous context establishes Momotaro's background and motivation for traveling to the Ogres' Island.

The event describes the hero's departure from his familiar environment to begin the journey or mission. The previous narrative context sets up Momotaro's adventure and provides background information on how he was born in a peach and is now setting out to get the Ogres' treasure.

events.name: Momotaro Meets Pheasant Ally
events.description: So Momotaro gave a millet dumpling to the monkey, and the two of them jogged on together. They hadn’t gone far when they fell in with a pheasant. “Ken! Ken!” said the pheasant. “Where are you off to, Momotaro?”
thoughts: The narrative has progressed from setting up Momotaro's origin and home life to preparing him for his journey. The old woman's discovery of the peach and its transformation into a baby boy is the pivotal event that sets the story in motion. Now, as Momotaro embarks on his adventure, he gives millet dumplings to the monkey, indicating their partnership. The encounter with the pheasant adds another character to the journey, further escalating the stakes and complexity of the narrative.

The event describes Momotaro making contact with his first ally, a monkey, as he embarks on his journey to the Ogres' Island. This is an important moment in the story, marking the beginning of Momotaro's interactions with other characters that will aid him in his quest.

actions: gave a millet dumpling to the monkey

events.name: Momotaro Meets Pheasant Ally
events.description: So Momotaro gave a millet dumpling to the pheasant, and the three of them jogged on together. They hadn’t gone far when they fell in with a dog. “Bow! Wow! Wow!” says the dog. “Where are you off to, Momotaro?”
thoughts: The text describes Momotaro giving millet dumplings to his animal companions as they journey together. This event is part of the preparation phase, where Momotaro acquires allies and support for his quest.

The event describes a friendly encounter between the hero Momotaro and a pheasant, where they jog on together after exchanging millet dumplings. This scene is part of the preparation phase as Momotaro gathers allies for his journey to the Ogres' Island.

actions: gave a millet dumpling to the pheasant
jogged on together with the pheasant
received a millet dumpling from Momotaro

events.name: Momotaro Wins Dog Ally
events.description: So Momotaro gave a millet dumpling to the dog, and the four of them jogged on together. By-and-by they came to the Ogres’ Island.
thoughts: The hero, Momotaro, is on a journey to the Ogres' Island and is gathering allies for his adventure. The event presents a series of encounters where he shares his millet dumplings with other animals, who then join him on his quest.

actions: gave a millet dumpling to the dog
jogged on together with the dog and other companions
received a millet dumpling from Momotaro
jogged on together with Momotaro and other companions

events.name: Momotaro Plans Attack Strategy
events.description: “Now, brothers,” says Momotaro, “listen to my plan. The pheasant must fly over the castle gate and peck the Ogres. The monkey must climb over the castle wall and pinch the Ogres. The dog and I will break the bolts and bars. He will bite the Ogres, and I will fight the Ogres.”
thoughts: The hero, Momotaro, and his animal companions plan their attack on the Ogres' Island. This event is part of the preparation phase, where the hero acquires allies and strategies for the main conflict.

The event describes Momotaro and his animal allies devising a plan of action before approaching the Ogres' Island. This planning is part of their preparation phase as they gather resources and acquire knowledge to aid in their mission.

events.name: Momotaro Prepares for Battle
events.description: Then there was the great battle. Japanese Fairy tale Series, 1 of 16. Griffiin, Farran and Co. Unknown author and illustrator. Published around the 1890’s.
thoughts: The narrative has progressed from a peaceful setting with an old couple to the birth of Momotaro and his subsequent journey. The current event describes Momotaro's preparation for battle against the Ogres on their island, gathering allies along the way.

actions: Prepares for battle against the Ogres

events.name: Pheasant Signals Final Approach
events.description: The pheasant flew over the castle gate: “Ken! Ken! Ken!” Momotaro broke the bolts and bars, and the dog leapt into the castle courtyard. “Bow! Wow! Wow!”
thoughts: The pheasant's call of 'Ken! Ken!' signals a turning point as Momotaro and his companions approach the Ogres' Island. They have just arrived at the final destination of their journey and are about to put their plan into action.
The pheasant's arrival marks a turning point as Momotaro's group forms and their plan of action begins to take shape.

events.name: Momotaro Defeats the Ogres.
events.description: The brave companions fought till sundown and overcame the Ogres. Those that were left alive they took prisoners and bound with cords—a wicked lot they were.
thoughts: The event describes a significant moment in the story where Momotaro and his companions fight against the Ogres. This is an action-oriented part of the narrative that drives the plot forward. The previous events have set up the journey to the Ogres' Island, and this event escalates the conflict.
The event describes a key moment in the story where Momotaro and his companions overcome the Ogres, but it's not a direct confrontation or a turning point. It's more of an intermediate stage.

actions: took prisoners and bound with cords

events.name: Momotaro Chooses Treasure Quest
events.description: “Now, brothers,” says Momotaro, “bring out the Ogres’ treasure.” So they did. The treasure was worth having, indeed. There were magic jewels there, and caps and coats to make you invisible.
thoughts: The event describes Momotaro's decision to leave and begin his mission to get the Ogres' treasure. This is a pivotal moment in the story where Momotaro takes initiative and sets off on his adventure.
The event describes Momotaro's decision to leave and begin his mission to the Ogres' Island. The decision itself is the event, not the departure.

The event describes Momotaro's decision to leave and begin his mission to retrieve the Ogres' treasure. The previous context establishes the hero's ordinary life, his motivation for going on an adventure, and his preparation. This event marks a turning point in the narrative as it sets the stage for the main conflict.

actions: decided to leave for the mission

events.name: Momotaro Shares Spoils
events.description: There was gold and silver, and jade and coral, and amber and tortoise-shell and mother-of-pearl. “Here’s riches for all,” says Momotaro. “Choose, brothers, and take your fill.”
thoughts: The event describes a key moment in the story where Momotaro's plan comes together and his companions help him overcome the Ogres. This is a turning point in the narrative as they successfully breach the castle and begin to achieve their goal.

events.name: Momotaro Assembles Allies
events.description: “Kia! Kia!” says the monkey. “Thanks, my Lord Momotaro.” “Ken! Ken!” says the pheasant. “Thanks, my Lord Momotaro.” “Bow! Wow! Wow!” says the dog. “Thanks, my dear Lord Momotaro.”
thoughts: The event describes Momotaro gathering his companions before beginning the battle against the Ogres. This is part of the preparation phase where he acquires what he needs — allies and a plan — to face the main antagonist.

The event shows the hero acquiring what they need — objects, knowledge, allies, or strategy — before facing the main antagonist. The story has progressed beyond setup and initial situation; the hero is now preparing for a journey, gathering companions and making plans to conquer the Ogres' Island.
